message:
fix(channel): correctness fixes for waker lifecycle, permit semantics, and evidence emission

broadcast: Guard SendPermit::send() against all receivers dropping
between reservation and commit — return 0 and skip enqueue instead of
writing an unobservable message to the buffer. The previous code would
increment total_sent and buffer the message even with receiver_count==0.
Also convert clear_waiter_registration() to panic on lock failure
(consistent with other lock callsites in the module) instead of silently
swallowing the error. Adds permit_send_after_last_receiver_drop_is_noop
test verifying delivered==0, total_sent unchanged, and buffer empty.

fault: Early-exit with SendError::Disconnected in FaultSender::send()
when the inner channel is already closed, BEFORE evaluating
reorder/duplicate/delay logic. Previously a fault sender with non-zero
reorder probability would buffer the value into its reorder queue and
then report success, even though no receiver exists to drain it. Adds
send_after_receiver_drop_with_reorder_returns_error test.

mpsc: Remove the SharedWaiter::queued AtomicBool field and all its
associated store/load calls. This flag was fully redundant — waiter
liveness is already determined by queue position (front of send_wakers)
and the lock-protected waker inside SharedWaiter. Removing it
eliminates a subtle ordering concern between the atomic and the mutex.
Also changes the waiter lock call in Reserve::poll from silent
if-let-Ok to expect(), matching the rest of the module. Moves the
AtomicBool import to the test module where it is still needed.

oneshot: Clear the registered waker when RecvFuture::poll detects
cancellation after a prior Pending result. Without this, the channel
inner retains a reference to a dead waker that will never be polled
again. Extracts TestNoopWaker into a named struct for reuse across
tests. Adds recv_cancel_after_pending_clears_registered_waker test
verifying waker presence before cancel and absence after.

partition: In heal_all(), emit per-directed-edge "heal" evidence
entries instead of silently clearing the partition set. Sorted edge
iteration ensures deterministic evidence ordering. In send(), move
record_drop() to only the Drop behavior path — the Error path now
emits "message_rejected" evidence (not "message_dropped") and does NOT
increment messages_dropped, since the message is returned to the caller
not lost. Updates partition_error_mode and heal_all_clears_all_partitions
tests to verify evidence content and stats correctness.

watch: Eagerly prune the receiver's waiter entry on Receiver::drop
instead of leaving a stale waker in the waiter list until a future
send or re-registration. Uses Arc::ptr_eq + strong_count to identify
the dead entry. Adds dropped_receiver_eagerly_removes_pending_waiter
test verifying waiter count drops to 0 on receiver drop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
